title: File metadata operations — stat, list_dir, mkdir, delete, rename
purpose: File metadata operations (stat, directory listing, creation, deletion, renaming) are needed by OTP's file and filelib modules. io_uring supports statx and unlinkat natively. Directory operations (opendir/readdir) are not directly supported by io_uring but can be submitted as thread-pool work via the ring's IORING_OP_POLL or delegated to the ThreadPoolRing fallback. This brief provides the metadata BIF surface that Gleam and Erlang code needs for file system interaction beyond read/write.
task: Implement file metadata BIFs: stat (file info), list_dir (directory listing), mkdir, delete (file and directory), and rename. Use io_uring statx for stat, and thread-pool delegation for directory operations.

requirements.title: file_info BIF (stat)
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:file_info/1 is called with a filename binary, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit a Statx operation to the CompletionRing and return a file_info record or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:file_info/1 registered as BIF with args (Filename :: binary())
Returns a tuple matching Erlang's #file_info record: {file_info, Size, Type, Access, Atime, Mtime, Ctime, Mode, Links, MajorDev, MinorDev, Inode, Uid, Gid}
Type atom: regular, directory, symlink, device, other
Access atom: read, write, read_write, none
Times as integer seconds since epoch
On error: {error, Reason} with errno mapping
Test: stat an existing file, verify size and type

requirements.title: list_dir BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:list_dir/1 is called with a directory path, THE SYSTEM SHALL return {ok, [Filename]} listing all entries or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: Uses blocking std::fs::read_dir on dirty scheduler thread (directory listing is not supported by io_uring)
Returns {ok, List} where List is a list of binary filenames (excluding . and ..)
Test: create temp directory with files, list_dir, verify all filenames present

requirements.title: mkdir, delete, rename BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide BIFs for creating directories, deleting files/directories, and renaming files.
acceptance: erlang:make_dir/1 — creates a directory, returns ok or {error, Reason}
erlang:del_file/1 — deletes a file, returns ok or {error, Reason}
erlang:del_dir/1 — deletes an empty directory, returns ok or {error, Reason}
erlang:rename/2 — renames a file or directory, returns ok or {error, Reason}
All use blocking std::fs operations on dirty scheduler thread
Test: mkdir, create file in it, rename file, delete file, delete dir

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement symbolic link operations (readlink, symlink) — future addition
SHALL NOT implement recursive directory operations — those belong in a library, not BIFs
SHALL NOT implement file permission changes (chmod, chown) — future addition
verification: cargo test passes with file stat returning correct metadata
Error cases (nonexistent file, permission denied) return correct atoms
